steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: Use the form $h(x)=a\cdot b^x$. The row $x=0$ gives $h(0)=a=1.36$. Use the row $x=2$: $$1.80=1.36b^2$$ $$b^2=\frac{1.80}{1.36}\approx1.324$$ $$b\approx1.15.$$ So the model is $h(x)=1.36(1.15)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the model against the table
steps.content: The first value confirms the coefficient: when $x=0$, $h(0)=1.36(1.15)^0=1.36$. The second value checks the base: $1.36(1.15)^2 \approx 1.80$. The third value also matches: $1.36(1.15)^4 \approx 2.38$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• $h \left(x\right)=1.15{ \left(0.36\right)}^{x}$: $1.15(0.36)^x$: $h(0)=1.15$, not $1.36$; and $0.36$ would shrink the values. Eliminated.
  • $h \left(x\right)=1.15{ \left(1.36\right)}^{x}$: $1.15(1.36)^x$: $h(0)=1.15$, not $1.36$. Eliminated.
  • $h \left(x\right)=1.36{ \left(0.15\right)}^{x}$: $1.36(0.15)^x$: correct starting value, but $h(2)=1.36(0.15)^2\approx 0.031$, nowhere near $1.80$. Eliminated.
  • $h \left(x\right)=1.36{ \left(1.15\right)}^{x}$: $1.36(1.15)^x$: $h(0)=1.36$, $h(2)\approx 1.80$, $h(4)\approx 2.38$. Matches all rows.
That matches the correct answer: D is correct.
